---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file. -->

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment  = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# partyscape

Diversity profiles, profile crossings, and beta diversity for
party-system research. Implements the three analytical tools proposed in
Neunhoeffer (forthcoming) on a small, tidyverse-free footprint.

## Installation

```r
# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("mneunhoe/partyscape")
```

## A 20-line tour

```{r demo, fig.show = "hold"}
library(partyscape)

# Two hypothetical systems with the same ENP but different structure.
a <- c(0.55, 0.15, 0.10, 0.08, 0.05, 0.04, 0.03)   # many small parties
b <- c(0.40, 0.30, 0.30)                           # three large parties

# 1. Diversity profile: D(q) from q = 0 (richness) to q = Inf (inverse of max).
dp <- diversity_profile(rbind(a = a, b = b))
dp

# 2. Profile crossings: where does one system overtake the other?
cr <- crossings(a, b, q = seq(0, 5, 0.01))
cr

# 3. Beta diversity across a country's election history.
history <- rbind(c(0.50, 0.30, 0.15, 0.05),
                 c(0.45, 0.30, 0.15, 0.10),
                 c(0.35, 0.30, 0.20, 0.15))
alpha_beta_gamma(history, q = c(0, 1, 2, 5))
```
